zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Agnes Daniel于1278年出生在英国越可爾时，她的父亲Adam Daniel33岁，母亲Agnes28岁。她和Sir John de Hotham II至少有3个儿子和1个女儿。她去世于1341年享年63岁。

nameMeanings.name: Daniel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, French, Spanish, Portuguese, German, Polish, Czech, Slovak, Romanian, Jewish, Assyrian/Chaldean, West Indian (mainly Haiti), and African (mainly Nigeria and Tanzania); Breton (rarely Le Daniel); Hungarian (Dániel): from the Biblical personal name Daniel, Syriac (Assyrian/Chaldean) Dānīʾēl, Hungarian Dániel (from Hebrew Daniyyel ‘God is my judge’), borne by a major prophet in the Bible. The chief factor influencing the popularity of the personal name (and hence the frequency of the surname) was undoubtedly the dramatic story in the Book of Daniel recounting the prophet's steadfast adherence to his religious faith despite pressure and persecution from the Mesopotamian kings in whose court he served: Nebuchadnezzar, Belshazzar, and Darius. The name was also borne by a 2nd-century Christian martyr and by a 9th-century hermit, the legend of whose lives were popular among Christians during the Middle Ages. Among Orthodox Christians in Eastern Europe the name was also popular as being that of a 4th-century Persian martyr, who was venerated in the Orthodox Church. In France, this surname is most common in Brittany.

Irish (Tipperary and Waterford): shortened form of McDaniel , which is actually a variant of McDonnell , from the Gaelic form of Irish Donal (equivalent to Scottish Donald), erroneously associated with the Biblical personal name Daniel. See also O'Donnell .
Americanized or Germanized form of Slovenian Danijel: from the Biblical personal name Danijel ‘Daniel’ (see 1 above).

variants.name: Daane
meanings: Dutch: from a short form of the personal name   Daniel  . Compare   Dane  .

variants.name: Dan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Ha To, Hieu, Lam, Chinh, Dung, Dung Anh, Long, Ly, Mai, Minh, Nga, Nguyen Van.  Romanian Corneliu, Silviu, Constantin, Dumitru, Ilie, Rodica, Stelian, Vasile.
Romanian; Hungarian (mainly Dán): from a short form of the Biblical personal name Daniel (Hungarian Dániel).
Romanian: from a short form of the Slavic personal names   Bogdan   and   Prodan  .

variants.name: Dacko
meanings: Polish: from a pet form of an Old Polish compound personal names beginning with Da- (for example, Dalebor), or of the Biblical names Daniel or Dawid (Polish form of David ).
Americanized form of Ukrainian Datsko: from a pet form of the personal name Danylo, Ukrainian equivalent of Daniel .

variants.name: McDaniel
meanings: Irish: altered form of McDonnell , itself an Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Domhnaill ‘son of Donal’, from association of the Gaelic patronymic with the Biblical personal name Daniel .

variants.name: Danel
meanings: French and Polish: from a variant of the personal name   Daniel  .

variants.name: Danca
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Nicola, Salvatore.
Portuguese (Dança): perhaps a nickname from dança ‘dance’.
Italian (D'Anca): probably a nickname from the vocabulary word anca ‘hip, haunch’.

variants.name: Dani
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Narendra, Saryu, Shashi, Bachu, Chandu, Devyani, Dilip, Dinesh, Hasit, Kaushik, Kirit, Manisha.
Italian, Romanian, and German; Slovak (also Dáni): from a short form of the personal name Daniel (Italian Daniele).
Albanian: from Dan (definite form Dani), a short form of the Muslim personal name Ramadan .

variants.name: Dagneau
meanings: French: variant, written under the influence of the word agneau ‘lamb’, of Daniau, which is from the personal name Daniau, a variant of Daniel . Compare Daignault , Daigneau , Daigneault , and Danyow .

History: The progenitor of the (majority of the) American Dagneaus was Michel Dagneau, sieur de Douville from Deux-Jumeaux in Calvados, France, who married Marie Lamy in Sorel, QC, in 1688. His descendants mostly bear altered forms of the surname, mentioned above.

variants.name: Dangel
meanings: German: from a vernacular form of the personal name   Daniel  . This surname is also found in France (Alsace).

variants.name: Danek
meanings: Czech (Daněk), Slovak, and Polish: from a pet form of Daniel or of any of the Slavic compound personal names formed with the element dan ‘given’ (see Dana ).
Czech (Daněk): nickname from daněk ‘fallow deer’.

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Agnes Daniel was born in 1278, in Yorkshire, England, her father, Adam Daniel, was 33 and her mother, Agnes, was 28. She had at least 3 sons and 1 daughter with Sir John de Hotham II. She died in 1341, at the age of 63.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Agnes Daniel est née en 1278, à Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Adam Daniel, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Agnes, avait 28 ans. Elle a eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille avec Sir John de Hotham II. Elle est décédée en 1341, à l’âge de 63 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Agnes Daniel 1278 in Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Adam Daniel 33 und ihre Mutter Agnes 28. Sie hatte mit Sir John de Hotham II mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1341 im Alter von 63 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes Daniel è nata il nel 1278 nel Yorkshire, England, sua padre, Adam Daniel, aveva 33 anni e sua madre, Agnes, aveva 28 anni. Lei avuto almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia con Sir John de Hotham II. Morì nel 1341, all'età di 63 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes Daniel(이)가 1278년 영국 요르크실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Adam Daniel(은)는 33세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Agnes(은)는 28세였다. 그녀는 Sir John de Hotham II(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1341년 63세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes Daniel nasceu em 1278, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Adam Daniel, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Agnes, tinha 28 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha com Sir John de Hotham II. Ela faleceu em 1341, com 63 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnes Daniel родилась в 1278 г. в местности еркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Adam Daniel было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Agnes, 28. У неё было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь от Sir John de Hotham II. Она умерла в 1341 г. в возрасте 63 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Agnes Daniel nació en 1278, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Adam Daniel, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Agnes, tenía 28 años. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija con Sir John de Hotham II. Murió en 1341, a la edad de 63 años.
